variants.label: Journey-led
variants.summary: Lead with the contributor's first successful docs workflow, then branch into repo vocabulary and deeper context.
tradeoffs: Fastest route from landing page to a useful page
Requires hand-curated outcome links to stay sharp

variants.label: Surface-led
variants.summary: Group the corpus by code surface first, with frontend, agent, and docs infrastructure sections.
tradeoffs: Better for maintainers who already know the repo layout
Slower for first-time readers who need task-oriented entry points

variants.label: Concept-led
variants.summary: Start with conceptual labels and protocols, then link into routes, commands, and the product surface.
tradeoffs: Can hide the fastest operational path when a reader just needs the next command

outcomes.label: Open the correct route
outcomes.summary: Start with the route split, local commands, and verification loop.

outcomes.label: Understand the browser boundary
outcomes.summary: See how `/`, `/docs`, and `/chat` divide reader and operator work.

outcomes.label: Decode seeded repo vocabulary
outcomes.summary: Read the labels that already exist in help text, tests, and seed data.

outcomes.label: Follow a context handoff
outcomes.summary: Use the protocol-style page shape for relay and handoff concepts.

journey.title: Add
journey.summary: Enter through the docs landing page and the first committed markdown pages.

journey.title: Check
journey.summary: Verify the active browser and MkDocs surfaces before making wider claims.

journey.title: Query
journey.summary: Use seeded labels and doc links to answer what each term means in this repo.

journey.title: Iterate
journey.summary: Use versioned labels, handoff pages, and diagrams to refine the docs set without breaking coherence.

personas.name: First-time contributor
personas.summary: Needs the shortest zero-to-aha path from a fresh checkout to a meaningful docs edit.
supportSignals: Which command proves the docs surface still works?

personas.name: Docs maintainer
personas.summary: Needs strong file-backed navigation, predictable templates, and evidence-driven update rules.
supportSignals: How do I keep the reader and MkDocs nav aligned?

personas.name: Operator
personas.summary: Needs to move between docs context and the `/chat` control surface without losing orientation.
supportSignals: Which route should I validate after changing markdown?

typography: Georgia-based editorial stack for long-form reading
Large hero type for route orientation
Monospace code blocks with explicit language captions
palette: Dark mode using slate and cyan accents
motion: Use hover elevation only for navigation cards
surfaces: Sticky sidebar for search and TOC

conventions: Start each page with an evidence-driven overview
Use headings that describe the next decision a reader needs to make
Prefer route, file, and command references over generic promises
Add related-page links instead of burying the next step in long prose
Keep diagram blocks in fenced code sections so both the web reader and MkDocs stay aligned

platforms.fumadocs: Card, Cards, Callout, Tab, Tabs, Step, Steps, Accordion, Accordions, TypeTable, ImageZoom, fumadocs-openapi APIPage

componentMappings.note: For Fumadocs output, the full component mapping in page-type-templates.json supersedes this coarse mapping.

fumadocsDesignTokens._note: These tokens supersede designSystem for Fumadocs output. Retain existing designSystem tokens for the landing page and MkDocs legacy output.

typography.heading: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif
typography._conflict_note: Fumadocs uses Inter (not Georgia). Georgia retained in designSystem for editorial/landing page use.

palette._conflict_note: Fumadocs uses blue (#2563eb) primary, not teal. Teal retained in designSystem for landing page brand identity.

fumadocsDesignTokens.ia_variant_note: Fumadocs docs use taxonomy-led IA (by page type). The journey-led IA in informationArchitecture is retained for the landing page navigation.

templateLibraryMappings._note: Maps old style-system template IDs to new page-type-templates.json page types.
